assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS for foreign exchange risk on an intragroup dividend.
The intragroup dividend exposure is being assessed only from the perspective of possible hedge accounting models, not detailed eligibility or effectiveness outcomes.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies a recognised asset or liability as a possible hedged item, which maps to the fair value hedge model for foreign exchange risk on a recognised dividend receivable/payable.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies a forecast transaction as a possible hedged item, which maps to the cash flow hedge model for foreign exchange risk on a forecast intragroup dividend.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct hedged item, which is a separate top-level hedge accounting model relevant to foreign exchange exposures in consolidation.
